etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 寺(寺) (sì) -> to hold
etymology.interpretation: The character 持(combines 手(手) (shǒu, "hand"), indicating the action of using the hand, with 寺(寺) (sì) as the phonetic component, providing the sound. The pictorial implication of a hand (手) suggests holding or maintaining something, leading to the sense of 'to hold; maintain; support.'

examples.chinese: 他总是持有乐观的态度(他總是持有樂觀的態度)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì chí yǒu lèguān de tàidù.
examples.english: He always maintains an optimistic attitude.

examples.chinese: 请持票排队(請持票排隊)。
examples.english: Please hold your ticket and queue.

examples.chinese: 她的观点很值得支持(她的觀點很值得支持)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de guāndiǎn hěn zhídé zhīchí.
examples.english: Her viewpoint is well worth supporting.
